title: Add reasons or examples  Explain why
prompt: Add reasons or examples : Explain why each suggestion is useful with a reason or real-life example.

title: Choose your story fast  Quickly pick
prompt: Choose your story fast : Quickly pick a clear memory or situation you want to talk about.

title: Add details Describe what you saw heard
prompt: Add details: Describe what you saw, heard, or felt to make the story more vivid.

title: Focus on some details Choose a few
prompt: Focus on some details: Choose a few important elements in the image to describe. You don’t need to cover everything—just focus on what stands out.

title: Build a picture Speak in a way
prompt: Build a picture: Speak in a way that helps the listener visualize the scene. Describe the setting, atmosphere, and layout of the image.

title: Describe peoples appearance actions and feelings Talk
prompt: Describe people’s appearance, actions, and feelings: Talk about what the people look like, what they are doing, and how they might be feeling based on their expressions or body language.

title: Ask yourself What are the people likely
prompt: Ask yourself: What are the people likely to do next? How might they feel afterward?

title: Compare the features and benefits of both
prompt: Compare the features and benefits of both options : Evaluate the advantages of each to determine which one better aligns with your preferences.

title: Plan strong reasons and supporting details to
prompt: Plan strong reasons and supporting details to explain why your option is better: Identify compelling arguments and examples that highlight why your choice is superior.

title: Use clear persuasive language and expand on
prompt: Use clear, persuasive language and expand on the information provided: Explain your reasoning clearly and confidently, adding details to strengthen your argument.

title: Clearly describe the issue or situation Give
prompt: Clearly describe the issue or situation: Give enough background so the listener understands what led to the decision.

title: Support your decision with clear and logical
prompt: Support your decision with clear and logical reasons: Explain why the decision was necessary, using facts or reasoning to strengthen your point.

title: Highlight the unique features of the image
prompt: Highlight the unique features of the image: Describe the aspects of the picture that stand out or are unusual.

title: Focus on what you can clearly describe
prompt: Focus on what you can clearly describe: Concentrate on the details you can explain well, making them vivid for the listener.

title: CELPIP Vs IELTS  Which English Proficiency
prompt: CELPIP Vs IELTS – Which English Proficiency Test is right for you?
